formats.format: $timestamp$ F5VPN session_id="$session_id$" user="$user$" client_ip="$client_ip$" start_time="$start_time$" event="$event$" device="$device$" message="$message$"

analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes F5 VPN authentication and session logs, capturing user login/logout events, session starts/ends, and authentication successes/failures. It standardizes F5 VPN security events into OCSF format with focus on network activity monitoring and access control
optimization_opportunities: Add input validation for IP addresses
Include error handling for malformed logs
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: f5_vpn-latest
-- OCSF Class: Network Activity (3005)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:18:00.159339

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local ACTIVITY_PATTERNS = {
    SESSION_START = {id = 1, name = "Start", status_id = 1, status = "Success"},
    SESSION_END = {id = 2, name = "Stop", status_id = 99, status = "Other"},
    LOGIN = {id = 1, name = "Logon", status_id = 1, status = "Success"},
    LOGOUT = {id = 2, name = "Logoff", status_id = 99, status = "Other"},
    AUTH_SUCCESS = {id = 1, name = "Success", status_id = 1, status = "Success"},
    AUTH_FAILURE = {id = 2, name = "Failure", status_id = 2, status = "Failure"},
    CONNECTION_FAILED = {id = 99, name = "Other", status_id = 2, status = "Failure"}
}

-- Local cache for frequent lookups
local empty_table = {}
local default_severity = {id = 1, name = "Informational"}

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize output structure with local variables for performance
    local output = {
        -- Mandatory OCSF fields
        class_uid = 3005,
        class_name = "Network Activity", 
        category_uid = 4,
        category_name = "Network Activity",
        type_uid = 300503,
        severity_id = default_severity.id,
        severity = default_severity.name,
        
        -- Initialize nested structures
        session = {},
        user = {},
        src_endpoint = {
            device = {}
        }
    }

    -- Efficient field mapping with validation
    local function safe_copy(src_field, dest_table, dest_field)
        if record[src_field] and record[src_field] ~= "" then
            dest_table[dest_field] = record[src_field]
            return true
        end
        return false
    end

    -- Copy core fields with validation
    safe_copy("session_id", output.session, "uid")
    safe_copy("user", output.user, "name")
    safe_copy("client_ip", output.src_endpoint, "ip")
    safe_copy("device", output.src_endpoint.device, "name")
    safe_copy("message", output, "message")
    safe_copy("start_time", output, "start_time")

    -- Activity name mapping with optimized lookup
    local activity = record.event and ACTIVITY_PATTERNS[record.event] or empty_table
    if activity then
        output.activity_id = activity.id
        output.activity_name = activity.name
        output.status_id = activity.status_id
        output.status = activity.status
    end

    -- Timestamp handling
    if record.timestamp then
        -- Assuming timestamp is in milliseconds
        output.time = tonumber(record.timestamp)
    else
        output.time = os.time() * 1000
    end

    -- IP address validation
    if output.src_endpoint.ip then
        local ip = output.src_endpoint.ip
        if not string.match(ip, "^%d+%.%d+%.%d+%.%d+$") then
            output.src_endpoint.ip = nil
            output.parse_error = string.format("Invalid IP address: %s", ip)
        end
    end

    -- Final validation
    if not output.activity_id then
        return nil, "Missing required activity mapping"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error handling wrapper
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string.format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

-- Optimization notes:
-- 1. Pre-compiled activity patterns reduce runtime lookups
-- 2. Local function definitions improve performance
-- 3. Early validation prevents unnecessary processing
-- 4. Efficient string handling with pattern matching
-- 5. Minimized table creation and modification
monitoring_recommendations: Track average processing time per event
